name: From the Lands of Alcarreria to
name_original: De las Tierras de Alcarreria a la Serrania de Cuenca — circular desde Peralveche
description: This moderate 41.4-mile mountain biking route from Peralveche explores the Tierras de Alcarreria and Serrania de Cuenca, featuring challengi
region_intro: Road cycling around Peralveche, situated in the Alcarria region of Guadalajara, Spain, offers routes through diverse landscapes. The area features extensive pine forests, picturesque valleys, and distinctive limestone formations such as the Tetas de Viana and Peña del Arco. Situated at an altitude of 1111 meters (3645 feet), the region provides routes with significant elevation changes, appealing to cyclists seeking challenging ascents and descents. Proximity to the Alto Tajo Natural Park further enhances the natural beauty and varied terrain available for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Peralveche The most popular road cycling route is From the Lands of Alcarreria to, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses the scenic Alcarria region, offering views of the characteristic landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cifuentes – Entrepeñas Viaduct loop from Mantiel, a difficult 75.7 miles (121.8 km) path. This challenging route includes substantial elevation gain, passing through varied terrain and offering distant views of the Entrepeñas Viaduct. Local road cyclists also love the Salmerón loop from Castilforte, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail leading through valleys and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Peralveche is defined by pine forests, valleys, and limestone formations, with routes offering considerable elevation changes. The network includes options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peralveche's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Peralveche?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes around Peralveche listed on komoot. These routes offer diverse experiences through the region's landscapes, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Peralveche?
A: Road cycling around Peralveche features significant elevation changes, with routes traversing extensive pine forests, picturesque valleys, and distinctive limestone formations. You'll encounter both challenging ascents and exhilarating descents, making it suitable for cyclists seeking varied terrain.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Peralveche offers routes with substantial elevation gain for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes – Entrepeñas Viaduct loop from Mantiel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 121.8 km path with over 1600 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find moderate road cycling routes around Peralveche?
A: Absolutely. Several moderate routes are available, perfect for those looking for a balanced challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018759379/from-the-lands-of-alcarreria-to" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Lands of Alcarreria to&lt;/a&gt; route is a popular moderate option, covering 66.6 km and traversing the scenic Alcarria region.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling near Peralveche?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see prominent landmarks like the distinctive twin limestone mountains of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. The unique natural rock arch of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5610581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Arco&lt;/a&gt; is another geological highlight. Some routes also offer distant views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting near Peralveche?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salmerón loop from Castilforte&lt;/a&gt; is a 75.9 km circular trail that leads through valleys and forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcantud – El Recuenco Start loop from Peralveche&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 37.5 km loop.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peralveche?
A: The Alcarria region, where Peralveche is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, making long rides through the forests and valleys more comfortable. Summer can be hot, while winter may bring colder temperatures and occasional precipitation.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate about the routes in Peralveche?
A: Road cyclists using komoot often praise the varied terrain, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the scenic views of the Alcarria landscape. The routes are appreciated for their mix of challenging climbs and rewarding descents, providing a stimulating experience for over 60 komoot users who have explored the area.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic viewpoints due to the region's elevated terrain. The area around the Tetas de Viana provides breathtaking panoramic views of the Alcarria Alta, Serranía de Guadalajara, and even the distant Sierra de Guadarrama. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mantiel and the Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What is the average distance and elevation gain for road cycling routes in this area?
A: The road cycling routes around Peralveche vary significantly. Distances range from approximately 37 km to over 120 km. Elevation gains can be substantial, with moderate routes typically featuring around 800-1000 meters of ascent, while difficult routes can exceed 1600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Is Peralveche a good starting point for exploring the Alto Tajo Natural Park by road bike?
A: Peralveche serves as a gateway to the Alto Tajo Natural Park. While specific road cycling routes directly into the park's deepest interior may be limited, the surrounding roads offer challenging rides through similar dramatic landscapes of canyons, gorges, and vast pine forests, characteristic of the broader Alto Tajo region.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of the region's water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes – Entrepeñas Viaduct loop from Mantiel&lt;/a&gt; provides distant views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. This reservoir is a significant feature in the landscape and can be a scenic point along your ride.
tips: Salmerón is a population that is located at the bottom of an area pocketed by hills and where a set of streams that come from the surrounding mountains circulate. The town is built around the church and its famous Callejón del Viento.
The parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción is the main monument of Valdeolivas. It is a Romanesque temple from the 12th-13th centuries that has a beautiful tower.
Road that meanders through what is called El Picazo, a mountain where the road has to be avoided in a rocky area with a very beautiful landscape!

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for road bikes?
faqs.answer: Despite being categorized for racebikes, the trail research indicates that this route, including features like 'first climbs and trails,' 'demanding climb,' 'dangerous descent with ruts,' and 'technical descents,' is primarily designed for mountain biking. It includes 'firebreak sections' and 'ancient trails' that would be unsuitable for standard road bikes. We recommend using a mountain bike for this adventure.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, featuring demanding climbs, technical descents with ruts, and sections that include firebreaks and ancient trails. It offers a true off-road experience through the Tierras de Alcarreria and Serrania de Cuenca regions.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this route is approximately 3 hours and 8 minutes, covering a distance of about 66.6 kilometers. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Peralveche. While specific parking details for Peralveche are not provided, it is generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the village or near the main access points to the trail.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass through the settlement of Salmerón, see the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, and traverse the El Recuenco Start mountain pass. These highlights offer glimpses into the local culture and natural beauty of the region.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty grade and descriptions of 'demanding climbs' and 'technical descents,' this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with mountain biking. Beginners might find certain sections quite challenging.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camino de Santiago de la Lana.BTT and the Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista, each covering about 7% of the route. It also touches upon other local paths like Camino Viejo de El Recuenco and Camino de la Fuente del Berro.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather, especially at higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned for dogs on this specific route, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near settlements, to ensure their safety and respect for the environment and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this circular route from Peralveche. However, always be mindful of private property and local regulations, especially when passing through villages or agricultural areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the region?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca is known for its diverse wildlife. Cyclists in the broader region often encounter deer, fallow deer, and roe deer. Keep an eye out for various bird species as well, especially in the forested and river valley areas.
